title: leucopogon ozothamnoides
intro: Leucopogon ozothamnoides, also known as the Swamp Apple-Berry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Epacridaceae. It is native to the coastal regions of southeastern Australia and Tasmania. The Swamp Apple-Berry has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Leucopogon ozothamnoides are typically made from the leaves and fruit of the plant, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures.
